data.content:
### Set Global Environment Variables on Sandbox Creation (JS/Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox/environment-variables/page.mdx

This code illustrates how to set global environment variables when creating a new E2B sandbox. These variables will be available for all subsequent operations within that sandbox. The `envs` option is passed to the `Sandbox.create()` method.

```js
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sandbox = await Sandbox.create({
  envs: { 
    MY_VAR: 'my_value',
  },
})
```

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

sandbox = Sandbox.create(
  envs={
    'MY_VAR': 'my_value',
  },
)
```

--------------------------------

### Set Environment Variables for Code Execution (JS/Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox/environment-variables/page.mdx

This example shows how to set environment variables specifically for a single code execution call within an E2B sandbox. These variables override any global variables with the same name for the duration of that specific command. The `envs` option is passed to the `sandbox.runCode()` method.

```js
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
const result = await sandbox.runCode('import os; print(os.environ.get("MY_VAR"))', {
  envs: { 
    MY_VAR: 'my_value',
  },
})
```

```python
sandbox = Sandbox.create()
result = sandbox.run_code(
    'import os; print(os.environ.get("MY_VAR"))',
    envs={
        'MY_VAR': 'my_value'
    }
)
```

--------------------------------

### Set Environment Variables for Command Execution (JS/Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox/environment-variables/page.mdx

This snippet demonstrates setting environment variables for a specific command execution within an E2B sandbox. Similar to running code, these variables are temporary and override global settings for the command's scope. The `envs` option is passed to the `sandbox.commands.run()` method.

```js
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
sandbox.commands.run('echo $MY_VAR', {
  envs: { 
    MY_VAR: '123',
  },
})
```

```python
sandbox = Sandbox.create()
sandbox.commands.run(
    'echo $MY_VAR',
    envs={
        'MY_VAR': '123'
    }
)
```

--------------------------------

### Command Environment Variables in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.4.0/commands/page.mdx

The `envs` property is a record of string key-value pairs representing the environment variables active when the command was executed. This is part of the `ProcessInfo` and details the command's environment.

```typescript
envs: Record<string, string>;
```

--------------------------------

### Authenticate E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/packages/cli/README.md

Logs in to the E2B CLI, establishing an authenticated session. This command is necessary to manage E2B sandboxes and templates. Authentication can also be done via an environment variable for non-interactive sessions.

```bash
e2b auth login
```

```bash
E2B_ACCESS_TOKEN=sk_e2b_... e2b template build
```

--------------------------------

### Configure API Key for Authentication

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.13.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Set the E2B API key for authentication purposes. If not provided, it defaults to the E2B_API_KEY environment variable.

```typescript
optional apiKey: string;

// E2B API key to use for authentication.
// Default: E2B_API_KEY // environment variable
```

--------------------------------

### E2B API Key Configuration

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.11.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Configures the E2B API key for authentication. It can be set directly or defaulted to the E2B_API_KEY environment variable.

```typescript
optional apiKey: string;
```

--------------------------------

### API Key Configuration

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.13.2/sandbox/page.mdx

Sets the E2B API key for authentication. This is a string option. Defaults to the E2B_API_KEY environment variable.

```typescript
optional apiKey: string;

// Default: E2B_API_KEY // environment variable
```

--------------------------------

### Default Environment Variables via Dot Files (Shell)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox/environment-variables/page.mdx

This command shows how to access default E2B sandbox environment variables when using the CLI. Instead of being accessible via SDK methods, they are presented as dot files within the `/run/e2b/` directory. This is useful for debugging or direct shell interaction.

```sh
user@e2b:~$ ls -a /run/e2b/
.E2B_SANDBOX  .E2B_SANDBOX_ID  .E2B_TEAM_ID  .E2B_TEMPLATE_ID
```

--------------------------------

### Set Environment Variables for Command in E2B SDK

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.9.0/commands/page.mdx

This option allows you to set environment variables for a command. It accepts a record of string key-value pairs. These variables override the default environment variables set in the `Sandbox` constructor. The default value is an empty object.

```typescript
optional envs: Record<string, string>;
// Default: {}
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize E2B Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-python-sdk/v1.7.3/sandbox/page.mdx

Initializes a new desktop sandbox instance. Allows configuration of resolution, DPI, display, template, timeouts, environment variables, and authentication details. Defaults are provided for many parameters, and environment variables can be used for API keys and domains.

```python
class Sandbox(SandboxBase)
def __init__(resolution: Optional[Tuple[int, int]] = None,
             dpi: Optional[int] = None,
             display: Optional[str] = None,
             template: Optional[str] = None,
             timeout: Optional[int] = None,
             metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
             envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
             api_key: Optional[str] = None,
             domain: Optional[str] = None,
             debug: Optional[bool] = None,
             sandbox_id: Optional[str] = None,
             request_timeout: Optional[float] = None,
             proxy: Optional[ProxyTypes] = None)
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize E2B Sandbox (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-python-sdk/v1.7.4/sandbox/page.mdx

Constructs a new E2B sandbox instance, allowing customization of resolution, DPI, display settings, template, timeouts, environment variables, and authentication details. It defaults to the 'desktop' template and uses environment variables for API keys and domains if not explicitly provided.

```python
class Sandbox(SandboxBase)

def __init__(resolution: Optional[Tuple[int, int]] = None,
             dpi: Optional[int] = None,
             display: Optional[str] = None,
             template: Optional[str] = None,
             timeout: Optional[int] = None,
             metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
             envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
             api_key: Optional[str] = None,
             domain: Optional[str] = None,
             debug: Optional[bool] = None,
             sandbox_id: Optional[str] = None,
             request_timeout: Optional[float] = None,
             proxy: Optional[ProxyTypes] = None)
```

--------------------------------

### Configure API Keys for E2B and Claude

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/code-interpreting/analyze-data-with-ai/page.mdx

Sets up environment variables with E2B and Claude API keys. These keys are essential for authentication and accessing the respective services. Ensure these are stored securely in a .env file.

```bash
E2B_API_KEY=e2b_***
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-***
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox API - List Sandboxes

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.1.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Lists all currently running sandboxes. Authentication can be provided via an API key or defaults to an environment variable.

```APIDOC
## GET /sandboxes

### Description
Lists all running sandboxes.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/sandboxes

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **api_key** (string) - Optional - API key to use for authentication, defaults to `E2B_API_KEY` environment variable.
- **domain** (string) - Optional - The domain to list sandboxes from.
- **debug** (boolean) - Optional - Enable debug mode for listing sandboxes.
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "api_key": "YOUR_API_KEY",
  "domain": "example.com",
  "debug": true,
  "request_timeout": 30.0
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandboxes** (array[SandboxInfo]) - List of running sandboxes.

##### SandboxInfo
- **name** (string) - The name of the sandbox.
- **id** (string) - The unique identifier of the sandbox.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandboxes": [
    {
      "name": "my-sandbox-1",
      "id": "sbx_123abc"
    },
    {
      "name": "another-sandbox",
      "id": "sbx_456def"
    }
  ]
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Set Custom Environment Variables for Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.13.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Define custom environment variables for the sandbox. These variables are used when executing commands and code within the sandbox and can be overridden during execution. Defaults to an empty object.

```typescript
optional envs: Record<string, string>;

// Custom environment variables for the sandbox.
// Used when executing commands and code in the sandbox.
// Can be overridden with the `envs` argument when executing commands or code.
// Default: {}

```

--------------------------------

### Set Command Environment Variables

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.9.1/commands/page.mdx

Defines environment variables for a command's execution. These variables override the default ones set in the Sandbox constructor. An empty object is used as the default.

```typescript
optional envs: Record<string, string>;

// Default
// {}
```

--------------------------------

### Connect to an Existing Sandbox (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Connects to a pre-existing sandbox using its unique ID. This allows interaction with the same sandbox from different environments. API key authentication is supported, defaulting to the E2B_API_KEY environment variable.

```python
@classmethod
async def connect(cls,
                  sandbox_id: str,
                  api_key: Optional[str] = None,
                  domain: Optional[str] = None,
                  debug: Optional[bool] = None)

sandbox = await AsyncSandbox.create()
sandbox_id = sandbox.sandbox_id

same_sandbox = await AsyncSandbox.connect(sandbox_id)
```

--------------------------------

### Custom Environment Variables for Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.13.2/sandbox/page.mdx

Allows setting custom environment variables for the sandbox. These variables are used when executing commands or code within the sandbox and can be overridden. This is a Record<string, string> option. Defaults to an empty object.

```typescript
optional envs: Record<string, string>;

// Default: {}
// Used when executing commands and code in the sandbox.
// Can be overridden with the `envs` argument when executing commands or code.
```

--------------------------------

### Connect to Existing Sandbox (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.2.0/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Establishes a connection to a pre-existing sandbox using its unique ID. This allows interaction with the same sandbox from different environments or applications. An API key can be optionally provided for authentication, defaulting to the E2B_API_KEY environment variable.

```python
@classmethod
def connect(cls, sandbox_id: str, api_key: Optional[str] = None, domain: Optional[str] = None, debug: Optional[bool] = None):
    # ... implementation details ...
    pass

# Example Usage:
# Assuming 'sandbox' is an existing sandbox instance
sandbox_id = sandbox.sandbox_id
same_sandbox = Sandbox.connect(sandbox_id)
```

--------------------------------

### Environment Variable Setup (.env)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/page.mdx

Example of how to store your E2B API key in a .env file for local development.

```bash
E2B_API_KEY=e2b_***
```

--------------------------------

### Get Sandbox Info

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.4.0/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Retrieves detailed information for a specific sandbox identified by its ID. Optional parameters include API key for authentication, domain for self-hosted environments, debug mode, request timeout, and custom headers. Default values for domain and debug mode can be set via environment variables.

```python
@classmethod
def get_info(cls,
             sandbox_id: str,
             api_key: Optional[str] = None,
             domain: Optional[str] = None,
             debug: Optional[bool] = None,
             request_timeout: Optional[float] = None,
             headers: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
             proxy: Optional[ProxyTypes] = None) -> SandboxInfo
```

--------------------------------

### Check if inside an E2B Sandbox (JS/Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox/environment-variables/page.mdx

This snippet demonstrates how to check if your code is running inside an E2B sandbox by accessing the E2B_SANDBOX_ID environment variable. It is useful for conditional logic based on the execution environment. The code uses the `Sandbox.create()` and `sandbox.commands.run()` methods.

```js
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
const result = await sandbox.commands.run('echo $E2B_SANDBOX_ID')
```

```python
sandbox = Sandbox.create()
result = sandbox.commands.run("echo $E2B_SANDBOX_ID")
```
